claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots and blocks PRD readiness on project mutations or missing evidence, with multiple independent agents (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) required for cross_check evidence policy.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates SHA256 digests of all project files except allowed write roots, using git ls-files and filesystem walk for comprehensive coverage
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit mechanism that captures pre-execution project state.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files outside allowed roots
evidence.why: This detects any project mutations during evidence agent execution.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: This shows boundary audit runs before agents to establish baseline.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit: snapshot after agents, diff with before, and assign project_mutation: violated | clean based on detected changes
evidence.why: This shows the boundary violation detection and reporting logic.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and override readiness to prd_ready: false
evidence.why: This is the key enforcement: boundary violations block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots: [repo-relative artifact dir]
evidence.why: This documents the boundary contract in the dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks for required evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) and blocks on EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED when any are missing
evidence.why: This enforces that all required evidence agents must complete before PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check evidence policy
evidence.why: This shows cross_check policy requires three independent agents.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code: false and explicit result_schema
evidence.why: This defines the three-agent evidence gathering system design.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks project facts that only have external/user/unknown evidence without project-scoped path evidence
evidence.why: This enforces that existing-project facts require project-scoped evidence with file locators.

evidence.summary: Execution guard: if (!execute || !allow) return result - dispatch requires explicit executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch flags
evidence.why: This shows evidence dispatch requires explicit authorization before running agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig configures Claude provider with agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full), disabling session persistence and customizing tool access via claude_tools settings
evidence.why: This shows full-tool agent configuration while maintaining boundary control.

evidence.summary: Doctrine: dispatch会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成readiness blocker
evidence.why: This documents the boundary audit design intent in Chinese.

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact shows boundary.project_mutation: clean, boundary.changes: [], proving boundary audit ran and detected no violations
evidence.why: This is real-world evidence that boundary audit works in practice.

evidence.summary: Same dispatch shows multiple blockers (EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED, EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING, EVIDENCE_AGENT_BLOCKED, EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT) keeping prd_ready: false
evidence.why: This proves readiness stays blocked on missing/incomplete evidence even with clean boundary.

evidence.summary: Same dispatch shows execution_policy.boundary with project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots restricted to evidence directory
evidence.why: This shows the boundary contract communicated to agents in practice.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes Claude with configurable timeout, config, cwd, and returns structured result with provider, exit code, stdout, stderr, timedOut status
evidence.why: This is the provider integration that evidence dispatch uses to run full-tool Claude agents.

assumptions: The dirty working tree mentioned in status_quo refers to uncommitted changes, not boundary violations
Recent dispatch artifacts represent typical boundary audit behavior, not edge cases
Cross-checker JSON parsing failures in recent dispatch are transient, not systematic issues
risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect certain file mutations (symlinks, permission changes, timestamps), agents could bypass detection
If allowed_write_roots configuration is incorrect, legitimate evidence writes could be flagged as violations
If provider adapter timeout or error handling fails, boundary violations might go undetected
If evidence agents ignore scope declaration rules, project facts could be supported by wrong evidence types

result.notes: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch system fully implements boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents. The boundary snapshot mechanism (buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots) captures SHA256 digests before/after agent execution and detects any project mutations outside allowed artifact roots. Violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that override PRD readiness to false. Cross_check evidence policy requires three independent agents (explorer/cross-checker/verifier), and readiness stays blocked until all complete successfully with valid evidence. Recent dispatch artifacts confirm boundary audit works in practice (project_mutation: clean) and that readiness remains blocked on missing/incomplete evidence even with clean boundaries.
